chapter_title.ar: بَابُ كَفَّارَاتِ مَا أَصَابَ الْمُحْرِمُ مِنَ الْوَحْشِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 109 – The expiations on what the one in Ihraam attains from the wild animals

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/374)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/374)

parts.text: أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ مُحْرِمٍ أَصَابَ أَرْنَباً أَوْ ثَعْلَباً قَالَ فِي الْأَرْنَبِ شَاةٌ.

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Al Hassanasws, said, ‘I asked himasws about the one in Ihraam killing a rabbit, or a fox. Heasws said: ‘Regarding the rabbit is (a penalty of) a sheep’.
en.sarwar: 8. Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr has narrated from abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who is in the state of Ihram and kills a rabbit or a fox. He (the Imam) said, ‘For a rabbit he owes one sheep.’”
